“Give me the information, so the grand jury comprised of regular Americans can say there’s nothing there,” Pirro said."},{"url":"https://apnews.com/article/feeral-reserve-trump-0fdd36447a6aa8ae3e7125930d03950f","title":"Federal judge quashes Justice Department subpoenas to Fed | AP News","content":"Judge James Boasberg said that a “mountain of evidence suggests” that the purpose of the subpoenas was simply to pressure the Fed to cut its key interest rate, as President Donald Trump has repeatedly demanded.

tavily.raw:
{"query":"Jerome Powell March 2026 grand jury subpoenas quashed Judge James Boasberg harassment pressure resign Federal Reserve news","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/amp/2026/03/26/fed-trump-jerome-powell-subpoenas-doj.html","title":"Federal Reserve says keep Jerome Powell subpoenas quashed","content":"Title: Federal Reserve says keep Jerome Powell subpoenas quashed
* The Federal Reserve's Board of Governors urged a judge to reject prosecutors' request that he reconsider his decision to quash subpoenas issued in a criminal investigation of Chair Jerome Powell. * U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro's prosecutors say they are investigating Powell over cost overruns in renovations of the Fed's headquarters in Washington and his congressional testimony about that project. * Judge James Boasberg, in blocking the subpoenas to the central bank, said the probe appeared motivated by Powell's refusal to bend to President Donald Trump's demands to cut interest rates quickly. The Federal Reserve Board of Governors urged a judge to reject prosecutors' request that he reconsider his recent decision to block subpoenas issued in a criminal investigation of Chair Jerome Powell over pricey renovations of the Fed's headquarters and his congressional testimony about that. The Fed's argument was expected, given that the board sought to block the subpoenas to the central bank in the first place, having said that they and the criminal probe were mere pretexts to get Powell to agree to cut interest rates more quickly and more sharply, as President Donald Trump has repeatedly demanded. Trump also railed on Thursday over the cost overruns of the Federal Reserve building's renovations, and griped that he was getting sued for demolishing the White House's East Wing to build a ballroom, while Powell appeared to be escaping legal liability. It is not clear when Boasberg will rule on the dueling motions, or whether U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro will drop her office's investigation of Powell if she loses her bid to get the judge to reverse his March 11 ruling. Boasberg, in his blistering ruling, quashed two subpoenas served by Pirro's prosecutors on the Fed's board, which sought records about the multi-billion-dollar project to renovate the central bank's headquarters, and Powell's testimony to a Senate committee in which he "briefly discussed those renovations.". "There is abundant evidence that the subpoenas' dominant (if not sole) purpose is to harass and pressure Powell either to yield to the President or to resign and make way for a Fed Chair who will," Boasberg wrote in the ruling.","score":0.8978479,"raw_content":null,"domain":"cnbc.com","popularity_score":11.052714492667349,"main_topic":"news"},{"url":"https://www.cbsnews.com/news/federal-reserve-jerome-powell-subpoenas-justice-department-judge?ftag=CNM-00-10aab4i","title":"Judge quashes subpoenas sent to Federal Reserve as part of DOJ's Powell probe - CBS News","content":"Title: Judge quashes subpoenas sent to Federal Reserve as part of DOJ's Powell probe - CBS News
# Judge quashes subpoenas sent to Federal Reserve as part of DOJ's Powell probe. *Washington —* A federal judge has quashed a pair of grand jury subpoenas sent to the Federal Reserve Board as part of a criminal probe by U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro's office, saying they were merely a pretext to pressure Chairman Jerome Powell into voting for lower interest rates or resigning. "There is abundant evidence that the subpoenas' dominant (if not sole) purpose is to harass and pressure Powell either to yield to the President or to resign and make way for a Fed Chair who will. On the other side of the scale, the Government has offered no evidence whatsoever that Powell committed any crime other than displeasing the President," Chief Judge James Boasberg of the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia wrote in his ruling, which was dated March 11 and unsealed on Friday. In January, Powell revealed that the Federal Reserve had received grand jury subpoenas from the Justice Department as part of an ongoing criminal investigation into him. The chairman — who has drawn President Trump's ire for declining to rapidly slash interest rates — said the probe centered on his comments about a years-long renovation project at the Federal Reserve's office buildings. In his ruling, Boasberg heavily quoted Mr. Trump, and said the motivation behind the criminal probe appeared to be driven by his desire to pressure the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates. "That is one of at least 100 statements that the President or his deputies have made attacking the Chair of the Federal Reserve and pressuring him to lower interest rates," the judge wrote, noting that when the pressure failed, he resorted to calling for Powell's ouster instead. Sen. Thom Tillis, a Republican from North Carolina who sits on the Banking Committee, said Boasberg's decision "confirms just how weak and frivolous the criminal investigation of Chairman Powell is and it is nothing more than a failed attack on Fed independence.". Powell's term is set to end in May, and Tillis has repeatedly said he would not support the confirmation of any of Mr. Trump's nominees to the Fed until the Justice Department closed its investigation into Powell.","score":0.8921218,"raw_content":null,"domain":"cbsnews.com","popularity_score":10.018025115699304,"main_topic":"news"},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/groups/OurColdwater/posts/3847509365544275","title":"Our Coldwater Plus | # **Federal Judge Quashes Trump DOJ Subpoenas Targeting Fed Chair Jerome Powell, Citing Pretextual Pressure Campaign** | Facebook","content":"Federal judge quashes Trump DOJ subpoenas targeting Fed chair. ### **Federal Judge Quashes Trump DOJ Subpoenas Targeting Fed Chair Jerome Powell, Citing Pretextual Pressure Campaign**. **WASHINGTON** — In a sharp rebuke to the Trump administration's Department of Justice, a federal judge has quashed grand jury subpoenas issued to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and the central bank's Board of Governors, ruling that the probe appeared to be a pretext aimed at pressuring Powell to lower interest rates or resign rather than a legitimate criminal investigation. Boasberg, chief judge of the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia, unsealed his opinion on Friday, March 13, 2026, declaring that the government had "produced essentially zero evidence to suspect Chair Powell of a crime." He described the justifications for the subpoenas as "so thin and unsubstantiated" that they could only be viewed as pretextual. Boasberg pointed to a "mountain of evidence"—including more than 100 public statements from President Donald Trump and administration officials—suggesting the subpoenas' "dominant (if not sole) purpose" was to "harass and pressure Powell either to yield to the President or to resign and make way for a Fed Chair who will" align with demands for aggressive rate cuts. The subpoenas, served in January 2026 by the U.S. Attorney's Office for the District of Columbia under Jeanine Pirro—a longtime Trump ally—sought records related to Powell's June 2025 testimony before the Senate Banking Committee. Powell, in a rare video statement released in January, had called the subpoenas "unprecedented" and part of an ongoing pressure campaign unrelated to the renovations themselves. In a fiery news conference following the ruling, U.S. Attorney Pirro blasted Judge Boasberg as an "activist judge" who had "neutered the grand jury's ability to investigate crime" and granted Powell "immunity" from scrutiny. Judge quashes subpoenas in Justice Department's investigation of Federal Reserve's Powell. Federal judge quashes Justice Department subpoenas of Fed Chair Jerome Powell. Judge quashes grand jury subpoenas of Fed, Jerome Powell in renovations probe. Court blocks probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell, DOJ to appeal. Judge blocks subpoenas against Fed Chair Powell.","score":0.8875269,"raw_content":null,"domain":"facebook.com","popularity_score":0.0,"main_topic":"news"},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/26/fed-trump-jerome-powell-subpoenas-doj.html","title":"Federal Reserve says keep Jerome Powell subpoenas quashed","content":"The Federal Reserve Board of Governors urged a judge to reject prosecutors' request that he reconsider his recent decision to block subpoenas issued in a criminal investigation of Chair Jerome Powell over pricey renovations of the Fed's headquarters and his congressional testimony about that. The Fed's lawyers, in a court filing unsealed Thursday, told Judge James Boasberg that the U.S. Attorney's Office for the District of Columbia failed to come even remotely close to meeting the legal threshold for its motion for reconsideration. The Fed's argument was expected, given that the board sought to block the subpoenas to the central bank in the first place, having said that they and the criminal probe were mere pretexts to get Powell to agree to cut interest rates more quickly and more sharply, as President Donald Trump has repeatedly demanded. "There is abundant evidence that the subpoenas' dominant (if not sole) purpose is to harass and pressure Powell either to yield to the President or to resign and make way for a Fed Chair who will," Boasberg wrote in the ruling.","score":0.88705814,"raw_content":null,"domain":"cnbc.com","popularity_score":11.052714492667349,"main_topic":"news"},{"url":"https://wltreport.com/2026/03/13/judge-boasberg-blocks-doj-investigation-fed-chair-jerome","title":"Judge Boasberg BLOCKS DOJ Investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell | WLT Report","content":"Title: Judge Boasberg BLOCKS DOJ Investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell | WLT Report
# Judge Boasberg BLOCKS DOJ Investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell. Today, he blocked U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro from investigating Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Specifically, he tossed out two grand jury subpoenas for records on the ballooning costs of Powell’s Federal Reserve renovations project. Watch what U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro had to say in response here:. 🚨 BREAKING: US Attorney Jeanine Pirro just announced activist Judge Boasberg has BLOCKED a Grand Jury from investigating the Federal Reserve. “As a result, Jerome Powell today is now bathed in IMMUNITY preventing my office from investigating the federal reserve. Today, an activist judge in Washington, D.C. has bathed Jerome Powell in immunity by taking the tool of a grand jury subpoena away from our ability to investigate the Federal Reserve.… pic.twitter.com/u6StlBURIu. Today, an activist judge in Washington, D.C. has bathed Jerome Powell in immunity by taking the tool of a grand jury subpoena away from our ability to investigate the Federal Reserve. In his ruling, Judge James Boasberg claimed Pirro had “no evidence” to justify an investigation. A federal judge has quashed a pair of grand jury subpoenas sent to the Federal Reserve Board as part of a criminal probe by U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro’s office, saying they were merely a pretext to pressure Chairman Jerome Powell into voting for lower interest rates or resigning. “There is abundant evidence that the subpoenas’ dominant (if not sole) purpose is to harass and pressure Powell either to yield to the President or to resign and make way for a Fed Chair who will. On the other side of the scale, the Government has offered no evidence whatsoever that Powell committed any crime other than displeasing the President,” Chief Judge James Boasberg of the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia wrote in his ruling, which was dated March 11 and unsealed on Friday. In January, Powell revealed that the Federal Reserve had received grand jury subpoenas from the Justice Department as part of an ongoing criminal investigation into him. The chairman — who has drawn President Trump’s ire for declining to rapidly slash interest rates — said the probe centered on his comments about a years-long renovation project at the Federal Reserve’s office buildings.","score":0.8805613,"raw_content":null,"domain":"wltreport.com","popularity_score":1.3121863889661687,"main_topic":"news"}],"response_time":2.44,"request_id":"994d80f0-ded5-4587-952a-d0aa440f3d35"}
